## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hey guys, Rucker here. In this video I want to talk about the default reply. Where to find it, how to use it and how to make sure it doesn't mess up with the current automations and flows. I'm going to share my screen and we're going to dive right in. The default reply lives in the automation section just below the main menu. And I'm going to quote many chat here. Default reply is sent when the bot doesn't know what to answer. The default reply is sent when the bot doesn't know what to answer. So the different scenarios here when a default reply can be triggered. The first one would be if somebody asks a question in your bot that is not picked up by, for example, keywords. You can set up keywords that have flows connected to them, which can trigger full automations. I will dive into that later on. The more common issue we see is that people break out of existing flows. And breaking out of a flow means nothing more than that somebody types in an answer to one of your questions versus replying to your question using a quick reply button or a regular button. For example, you want to know what somebody's favorite color is. And you give them five options, red, blue, green, purple and yellow. Instead of that the user now steps one of these options. They type in blue. But the keyword blue is not configured. And it's not connected to a quick reply button or a normal button. So in this case, the bot receives a user input, but it doesn't know what to do with it. That is when the default reply will actually trigger. Now the tricky thing is that the default reply is a standard message. It's really difficult if not undurable to create a default reply that actually taps in to the user input. That is one of the reasons why I don't like to use the default reply because it's too broad. And it's often not on par with what the user actually was doing. Nevertheless, I want you to know what it is and how you could configure it. Now the standard default reply message by Managed is a little bit random in my opinion because it says it will reply with bots and ads typically replies in one day. If you want to ask something, just press talk to a human. Now if somebody broke out of a flow, this isn't necessarily a relevant message.

## [03:04 - 05:57]

So what you could do, and I added this message here, just change the starting step there. And we're going to dive into how we built this flows and messages later on. But for example, if the bot, if you want to use the default reply and you want to message something that says, I would say something like, hey, Rutger, I did not fully understand your question. I'm still learning. Tap on one of the buttons below to let me know how I can help you. And then these buttons could would tap into the most common questions you get. So for example, a store locator or availability or opening hours. And I just added this as an example, but this then would add to a new message where you will probably have a link to a third party website or your own website where people can find, look up the store. Again, we will dive into how we will use quick replies and buttons to trigger new messages. And I said before, I don't actually use the default reply button. But again, I just want you to know that it's there. If you would decide to use the default reply button, my advice would be to set it to send once per 24 hours instead of every time. Because what will happen if you set it to every time, as soon as somebody is, a user is confused with the messages they are getting from your bot and they keep typing and they keep replying every time they will send a message to your bot, it will trigger the same default reply. So be aware of that. Somebody just starts a conversation that is not configured in your bot, your bot is not ready to get into that conversation. And we will dive into how to create actual conversations later on the course using artificial intelligence, which is pretty awesome. But before we get there, this will happen, people will start messaging and they are confused, so they will send more messages. Deflate reply will trigger over and over and over again. Again, be aware of this. I don't use it in all my builds. I have it disabled and I will show you different scenarios and different flows and different best practices, how to catch people, how to build that safety net for people who are breaking out of a flow or who are sending messages to your page without being in a sequence or a flow. I hope this makes sense. If you have any questions about the default reply, just send us a message and we are happy to talk to you about it and see how we can out here.
